mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive 20d returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5d returns occur in less than 50% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in more than 55% of cases or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth drives positive equity returns only when inflation remains subdued, as rising output without price pressures expands real corporate margins over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.4% monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth that immediately boost equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in less than 52% of months with payroll gains or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling industrial production indicates inventory drawdowns that precede restocking cycles and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The GDP-INDPRO spread fails to predict 60d returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 or yields negative returns in more than 60% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0% triggers margin compression for highly leveraged firms and reduces equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative 20d returns occur in less than 50% of periods above the threshold or the signal loses predictive power during rate hike cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal consumption expenditures directly increase corporate revenue expectations and drive positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in less than 53% of months with consumption growth or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.
